archetype_reasoning: 449 resolved geo/elections trades. Iran_ME dominant (392/449 = 87%). Overall accuracy: 50.6% — near-random despite high trade count. Avg price: 0.236 — consistently buying the low-probability/cheap side. Contested entries: 65.5% (294/449). Near-cert entries: 3.6%. PNL = -$25,145 (significant losses). Iran_ME accuracy: 51% on 392 trades — near-random despite the domain concentration. Trump_US: 45% on 76 trades — below chance. The combination of 449 geo trades, 65.5% contested entry rate, 50.6% overall accuracy, and -$25K PNL represents no demonstrated forecasting edge. The trader appears to follow a systematic strategy of buying low-probability positions at cheap prices (avg 0.236), but the near-random outcome distribution and significant losses confirm this approach carries no predictive signal value. High volume in Iran_ME without accuracy above chance qualifies as VOLUME_SPECIALIST. The negative PNL despite reasonable (50.6%) accuracy suggests unfavorable position sizing or systematically overpaying even at 'cheap' prices. PNL=-$25,145 technically passes STR-003 PNL filter (> -$100K) but represents a significant loss. Confidence LOW: further monitoring needed to verify near-random accuracy is stable, not recent deterioration.

signal_weight_reasoning: Near-random accuracy (50.6%) and negative PNL (-$25K) provide no basis for DOMAIN_ONLY or FULL weight. MINIMAL is the conservative starting floor. Given near-random accuracy and losses, the honest assessment is that this trader's positions carry no systematic signal value at this time. Human review required to determine if recent periods show improvement.
